title: Fitting a letterbox
subtitle: Cut the slot in the door, set the plate, seal the draught excluder
excerpt: How to fit a standard letter plate to a timber front door: marking the cutout to the correct position, cutting the slot with a jigsaw, fitting the plate with the securing bolts, and fitting the draught-excluding flap.
sourceNotes: Collins Complete DIY Manual. British Standards BS EN 13724 (letter-plate dimensions).

content.text: is a straightforward job on a solid timber door. On a UPVC or composite door, the cutting requires different tools and methods; this tutorial covers the timber door only. Most letter plates include a template; use it rather than transferring measurements directly from the plate, as the visible plate is always larger than the cutout.

The standard position is centred horizontally on the door, at a height of 700 to 1,000 mm above the floor. Position it in the middle rail of a panelled door, not cutting through a stile or the thin panel sections. Avoid cutting close to the edge of any panel.
Tape the cutting template to the door face at the chosen position. Mark around the slot cutout with a pencil. Mark the two bolt-hole positions.
Step 2, drill starter holes at the corners.
Drill a hole at each corner of the marked slot, sized to accept the jigsaw blade. Drill a pilot hole at each bolt-hole position.

Insert the jigsaw blade into one corner hole. Cut along the marked lines to remove the slot waste. Keep the saw moving steadily and the baseplate flat on the door face. Sand the cut edges smooth with 120-grit.
Apply two coats of exterior primer to the raw cut edges inside the slot before fitting the plate. A primer applied to the slot walls seals the end grain against moisture.
Step 5, fit the outer plate and fixing bolts.

Feed the fixing bolts through the plate from the outside face. Feed them through the door at the bolt holes. Fit the inner cover plate and tighten the bolts from inside the door using a screwdriver. The plates should pull tight against both door faces without rocking.

attrs.fix: Fit a draught-excluding box behind the inner cover plate. Check the inner flap spring tension; most letter plates allow the flap spring to be adjusted.
attrs.cause: Draught-excluding sleeve not fitted, or the inner flap is not seating against the face properly.
attrs.symptom: Draught around the letterbox after fitting

attrs.fix: Apply a thin coat of petroleum jelly to the hinge pin. If the flap itself has warped, replace the letter plate.
attrs.cause: The flap has warped or the spring has stiffened in the cold.
attrs.symptom: Outer flap will not close in cold weather
